taxonomy: Thlaspi schudichii is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a member of the genus Thlaspi, which contains several species of plants commonly known as penny cresses. The Brassicaceae family includes a wide variety of plants such as cabbage, broccoli, and mustard. Thlaspi schudichii is native to Europe and is often found growing in a variety of environments. The plant's classification is further broken down into its kingdom, which is Plantae.

intro: Thlaspi schudichii, also known as blue rock cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health and has been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including the alleviation of coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Thlaspi schudichii include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thlaspi schudichii is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and expectorants, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects.
